edus.text: Have you used spotify?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I tried it out once, but didn't stick with it. I liked that it's basic features were free. I couldn't find songs I liked though
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah there is  a lot of songs on the service. Did you know that is has over 4 million songs that haven't even been played once? That's apparently 20 percent of their songs!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That is crazy! 4 million unplayed! With so many millions, I should give it a try again. Maybe I should try the paid subscription, with improved streaming quality?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: It might help, but with so many unplayed songs it may be an indicator there's too many. After all, it takes 130,745 plays per day for a musician to earn minimum wage on the site.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That's a shame for the musicians. I 'm surprised Google hasn't come up with competitor yet. Did you realize Google is over 20 years old now. It was founded in 1998!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Actually I did know of that, but did you know that google's map changes depending on the country you live in due to boarder disputes?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Interesting. Probably saves them from getting hate mail! Hard to believe it started with just two guys, Larry Page and Sergey Brin. They only own 14% of the shares now, though.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah, but I'm sure that's quite substantial considering how big they are. They went down once in 2013 and decrease all web traffic by 40 percent!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That's nice. It's nice, too, that they at least pretend to care about the earth. They once used live goats to mow the lawns around the headquarters.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: That's definitely a good thing, considering 80 percent of earth's natural forest's have been eradicated.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I wonder how that looks on Google maps! Only 29% of the earth is even land.  The other 71% is water.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Ah yes that is true isn't it? Well I must go now, good talking to you.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: This conversation was so short! Reminds me of how if the earth's history was a 24 hour period, humans would only represent 1 minute and 17 seconds!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Oh wow, that puts things into perspective.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Such a short period, and so much has changed on earth in that time. Days used to be only 5.5 hours long when earth was first formed.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Crazy to think we went from that to now facing the complete elimination of helium in our atmosphere
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I hope when extraterrestrials come they don't need the helium! Maybe we should talk to the United Nations' Ambassador to extraterrestrials. Can you believe that's a real job?!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I had no idea, how strange!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: It sure is! I guess maybe they'll show up some time, even though earth is the only astronomical object known to harbor life.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Ah yes, but who knows considering the vastness of space? Had an interesting conversation, good day.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Are you a fan of YouTube?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Hi there, I am, I use it all the time for music and if I ever need to learn how to do something.  How about you, do you use it much?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I love YouTube! Honestly, I use it mostly for vines and funny videos, but I occasionally do watch a good how-to video! Did you know it was founded by three former PayPal employees?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yeah the how-to-videos are lifesavers sometimes!  No, I didn't know that, very interesting though that finance company employees would start a video page.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Agreed! And it's the second-largest online search engine, so much more than just a video page!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yeah it really is, and I think it is owned by Google now so those two make up two internet monsters.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah, I think they make up a really significant portion of internet traffic online. I know Netflix and Youtube make up half internet traffic in North America during peak times!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yeah I think that Netflix peak time is just crazy for internet traffic!  Good call.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: There are so many good movies on Netflix. Did you know The Shining is on Netflix? I heard the young boy, Danny, didn't know he was acting in a horror movie until years later!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I think I have read about that before too funny!  I'm sure they didn't want to scare him but I wonder what he thought the movie he was helping film was about?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Wouldn't it be kind of sad if he didn't even realize he was in a movie? Like if he thought that was just real life and he was being told what to say and do. That would be super scary!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That would be!  I know some of the scenes in that movie for sure would scare me a bit.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yes, like the wave of blood coming out of the elevators or the twin girls (though they weren't in the book I think. I didn't like the movie compared to book, but it was cool at least that they made a movie at all. Apparently watching a scary movie like The Shining can burn up more than 180 calories!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That is wild!  But, some people get really scared and I guess that gets the old heart beating pretty quick!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yes that's probably it! Did you know that Stephen King (author of the Shining) actually considers Bambi to be a horror movie?? He calls it the first horror movie he ever saw.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I wonder why he would ever say that.  I do know that movie scared children when the mother gets killed, but I don't know about mr. King.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah well he was young when he saw it, too. It probably stuck with him a while. I wonder if that experience influenced some of the books/movies he's created!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That is a good point, you are probably right.  Do you know that the instrument that makes all those metal sounding noises in horror movies is called?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah, I heard it was called a waterphone! Did you know that if you watch a horror film before viewing abstract are you actually enjoy the art more?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: No, but why would anyone have tested or ever thought of that theory is what gets me.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah that's crazy! Well, it was very nice talking to you! I'm going to go watch The Shining right now!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Hey there do you ever play golf? I personally think it's really boring
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yes, I  live on a golf course, lol.  I enjoy it,have friends that got me started. but I use to think it was boring,especially to watch on TV, the voices would put me to sleep!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I agree with you! Do you know how much water your golf course uses by the way? Apparently the average golf course uses about 300,000 gallons per day
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I know that is alot! Its usually recycled, doesn't smell that great.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Oh I didn't know that. But again I haven't really played, so I guess I wouldn't know that LOL. It's crazy that the golf course in Dubai, Saudi Arabia, uses for million gallons a day, but that is a desert so it makes sense
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I know, that place doesn't care about how much anything is.lol Did not know Babe Ruth played.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: No I didn't know that either. You was actually a very famous golfer, although I only know him from playing baseball. Do you like Samuel Jackson by the way?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yes, I do! I think golf can be very relaxing for men and women. Its fun with all your friends. You might like it someday!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Haha yes, my friends always try to get me into it, but I just can't bring myself to play. But I will probably play one day haha. But yes Samuel Jackson loves Golf and actually puts it in his contract that he can play twice a week
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I saw that, compared to the size of the United States, Rhode Island and Delaware aren't very big. Its good exercise if you walk, lol most people use the cart.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Haha yes I know that my friends use cards LOL so not necessarily go to walk for me. how often to use the Internet by the way? I know I use it right now and I'm using it almost everyday
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Alot more than I should. I'm on it all the time. If not at home, or my phone. I watch no reg TV anymore. I watch all my shows on the internet now. I do like that.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yes I agree with you. I don't even have cable personally. I do like knowing that I can get 3-g service on the peak of Mount Everest though. I definitely would post on Facebook LOL
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That's interesting! Oh, I guess the constant clicking is what makes the addiction.  Crazy.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I probably have that haha. I know I like to click links and go exploring around the internet. But I don't ever read terms of service though. Apparently only 7% of people do
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I believe the 7%. They make those terms so detailed and long, no one wants to read all that!  Who knows what they put in there!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I agree with you! And I've never read it so I wouldn't even know haha. I do like pressing the hamburger button though, have you heard about the hamburger button that you see online? The three horizontal lines?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I use that all the time, I didn't that was the name! Why hamburger?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I guess it looks like a hamburger? top and bottom line would be a bun, and I guess the middle line would be the Patty LOL? I don't know but it makes me hungry
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I know, Lets hope the internet doesn't burn down so we don't have to go to the Library of Alexandria! lol
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: LOL I hope you're right! I would not be able to get about my day without a working internet haha., especially I be missing out on things like email and file sharing
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Do you have a favorite animal?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I do, dogs are my favorite by far, what about you? out of the 7 million species which one is your favorite one
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I love dogs, they are amazing. There are 20 quintillion individual animals. Do you own one?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I do own one, his my best friend, and that is amazing, didnt know they were so many of them.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I love dogs! what is his name?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: His name is Spoty, do you have a pet? animals that are domesticated develop a white patch in their foreheads
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: awww I had a dog named Rocky, sadly we had to put him down after 16 years. Haven't got one since though. i never knew about the white patch, i wonder why
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That is sad, that must had been really painful Im sorry for your loss, and I think its like an evolutionary thing.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: For sure, i still miss the little rat terrier, but great memories! Have you heard how hiccups formed?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: No, how were they formed? I do hate them wish we wouldnt have those.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: They occurred over time when early land animals had both gills and lung
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Thats interesting, we sure live in a cool planet, crazy how the earth got formed over 4.5 billion years ago, its been a long time since then.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Right, that is so crazy to think. When it first started there was only 5.5 hours in the day
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: It is crazy, the earth has changed a lot since then, I wonder how it will change in the future. Human are abusing the planet and that really worries me.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Right, I heard that like 80% of forests have already been destroyed sadly
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yeah 80% of the, and we only represent 1 min and 17 seconds of the history of earth.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: wow, that is crazy to think about. They need to do more to protect the last 20%
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I agree, the earth will also be helium free by the end of this century I wonder if the human race is also responsible for that.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Wow, what will they do about balloons lol. It was nice chatting with you!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I know right many kids and clowns will be disappointed at us and our generation, it was great chatting with you as well have a great night bye.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Have a great night also! bye!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Have you ever visited Washington DC?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Good morning, yes, I have when I was a kid I visited D.C. on a school trip.  Have you?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: No I have not but would like to some day, were you able to notice that the guest house is actually bigger than the White House?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: No, I don't think we visited the  "guest house"  but, I was pretty shocked to see the White House isn't all that big.  I've seen private homes around my town that are much larger than it.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Oh wow, it does look quite "grand" on TV but if they're still using floppy disks to store files, I'm sure much of the other things are pretty outdated as well.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That's funny, I can't believe they are still using the floppies.  I would think that would be a security risk.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: A huge security risk or maybe not considering how backwards it is and no one has access to those things except the government, I suppose.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That's true, it may be pretty smart on their part. Did you know it took the secret service 4 days to determine the White House had been struck by bullets on November 11, 2011?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: That's crazy! I wonder why it took them that long to do that, but then again, a man in France impersonated to be a government official and managed to sell the Eiffel Tower twice! Who were the buyers?!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I know, wonder how they missed the bullets whizzing around? Amazing, they could get away with selling the tower, not once but twice!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Exactly! That's like selling the Statue of Liberty! Someone might have done it or gotten close like that man and it might even be in Wikileaks which the government plans to destroy.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I had heard that Wikileaks had published the government's plans to destroy it! You never know what the government is up to.  Did you realize they own 86% of the land in Nevada.  What's going on out there?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Exactly, perhaps we will find out in Wikileaks, but I wonder if the government owning that much land impacts real estate rates significantly, any ideas?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Probably, drives the prices down, I would think.  Have you heard about the Norwegian government donating a billion dollars to help save the Amazon rainforest?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: That's very commendable, I wonder if the US has contributed to efforts like that but I don't think that is likely with the current president. What do you think?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: No, I'm sure Trump hasn't allowed any of that!  Things would surely have been different if Hillary had won the election.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I think so too, but I didn't realize she had a Grammy to her name, it's for best spoken word album for a book, I didn't even know that was a category!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: For the audio recording of her book, " It Takes A Village".  I didn't know there was such a category, either.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah, I wonder if that category still exists. I wonder if Hilary Clinton will be running as the Deomcratic Party nominee for the next election?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I think her time has passed, but the Democrats have really no clear front runner out of their pack out there now.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I think Trump might take credit for setting that precedent sadly.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Are you fan of Gruden and the Raiders?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Don't know much about them. The Raiders are a football team?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yes. The other Gruden, Jay, is not doing well in Washington either.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: His older brother is named Jon? He just got a job with the Raiders?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yes. I think both of them might be replaced next year.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: It's a competitive sport. There is no rule against women in the sport. I wonder when they will show up.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Probably not for awhile. Maybe one of them could take the Redskins coaching job.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I thought the Redskins were supposed to change their name because of the controversy.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I think the league got past that but are still dealing with poor seasons due to poor coaching
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: The earnings of players are not as high as I thought. An engineer can make more over a career.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I think engineers certainly deserve that kind of pay.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: The average NFL games has only 11 minutes of live gameplay. Less than I expected.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah, the league would rather show commercials to make money.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: The only nonprofit team left is Green Bay. I wonder how much money they pull in during the season. They are worth $2.35 billion.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah, they are the face of the league when it comes to a community owned team
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I didn't know the origin of their name: The Indian Packing company.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Never heard of them. Who are you rooting for in the Super Bowl?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I haven't thought about it. I usually want the underdog to win. I like close games, too.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: This years 17 week season has come to a close and now its playoff time
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Who are you rooting for?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Saints or Patriots.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Hello,  do you watch Game of Thrones?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I do, I can't wait for it to start in april, what about you?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Same!  I still cant believe this is the last season,  but I am glad this story will come to an end.  I wonder what spin off they will do,  I would like to see a series on the Tagaryen line.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: ya I can't wait either! what is crazy is that harry lloyd, the guy who played a targaryen is actually related to charles dickens, thats wild!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: That is crazy!  I really hated his character though lol Viserys Targaryen.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: oh ya I didn't like that guy either haha, also there is a character on game of thrones related to charlie chaplin, lots of talented-related people on that show for sure!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: yeah I read that too,  I dont know that character though.  Who is your favorite GOT characters?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: ya I don't know that character either, well for the uncommon characters I really like Tywin Lannister, he is so awesome and ruthless, but dead unfortunately haha, what about you
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: My favorites are Tyrion Lannister and Arya Stark.  Uncommon character I like Oberyn Martell.  But he died too.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: ya all the best characters are dying! did you hear the rumor that little finger is still alive? he used a shapeshifter and that was who was killed, that is the rumor
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yes I did hear that theory,  that would be crazy.  I know right, grrm really likes to kill off our favorite characters!  I am kinda scared my faves are gonna get killed off in this season.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: haha I know, if they kill too many people then I won't have anyone to root for, did you know Arya is the fastest rising baby name in the usa?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I believe it.  It is really popular,  but I seen mostly Aria,  different spelling.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: ah I haven't met one yet, did you know they film GOT mostly in ireland? that's why it looks so foreign to me I guess
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah My aunt and cousin went there in the summer and took pics at the sites and brought me back a bunch of souvenirs lol Definitely on my bucket list now!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: well thats lucky! have you heard of the show game of lions? it follows a pack of lions as they live in the wild haha, probably lots of killing haha
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: yes I have I have only watched 2 episodes though.  I love those animal shows.  It was ok,  I like ocean stuff better.  Its funny that they chose a lion show though lol
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: haha ya of all animals, do you eat fast food? i guess the drive through was invented in 1975, would have thought it happened earlier personally
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Not really anymore,  except french fries!  The drive thru is an amazing invention.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I want some right now! they should make a show like game of thrones but call it game of fast food, where fast food companies battle! but if you've seen Demolition Man then you already know that Taco Bell wins the franchise wars!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Haha I have seen that movie. "All restaurants are Taco Bell"  lol Its been great chatting,  have a good one.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Are you a football fan? I would love to see a female player soon in the NFL since there is no rule against it!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I follow football, but I'm not fanatical. I am all in favor of female players.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: How do you feel about the Saints cheerleaders having to leave a restaurant if a player walks in? That seems so wrong and olden days to me!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I don't like it. I understand they don't want them mixing with the players but I think that's really extreme.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: That's super extreme. It's also extreme that players get paid as much as they do and only play 11 minutes per game at the most!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I know that's crazy. But let me ask you, why do we watch?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Because it's fun. I don't know that I like the tracking chips in the players and ball. It's fine if they just use it for coaches knowledge, etc., but I hope they don't start to use it like replays.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I don't think they should use them. Someone will find a way to use them to cheat.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I agree, just like the Patriots have been accused of cheating.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Agreed. They could use the chips to see how many times the ball rotates and then let a little air out.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Any idea what a lannister is? That's the word used to describe the Patriots by the game of thrones author...
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I have no idea. But, don't you think Brady and Belichick make a good team. I think they would trip their own mothers if it would help them win.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: They would... I knew Brady was a righty, but didn't know there is only 1 left handed quarterback in the NFL. I thought there would at least be a few.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That is a surprise. I suspect it's because the ends get use to the ball coming at a specific angle and would throw them off.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Did you know the huddle started with a deaf quarterback, Paul Hubbard, not wanting other teams to see his hand signals? Crazy since hand signals are used all the time now out in the open!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yeah I think I have to question where they came up with that piece of trivia. Do you know what halo is?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: The video game? I heard Baker Mayfield is good at it and almost became a competitive gamer!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That's what I heard but I'm not familiar with the game. What's it about?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I think it's a combat game, but not entirely sure. I'll go check it out!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Okay thanks. I think we're running out of things to talk about. Maybe we should call it a night.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: This is going to take me awhile to figure out Halo. I'll talk to you later!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Hey there have you ever played golf before?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yea a couple times but I always end up shooting into bunkers and water hazards, you?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Haha yes I'm not very good. It's very crazy that the whole is only about 4 and 1/4 inches in diameter yes people can get a hole-in-one with that tiny little ball haha
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yea but there are a bunch of golf fanatics especially in the US. The average US course consumes 312  thousand gallons of water a day
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah that seems like a waste to me. It's also a waste that in Dubai the golf courses can use up to four million gallons of water a day, I can think of a lot better use for that water
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yea it's quite shocking Dubai uses that much water for courses considering they are in a middle of a desert
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yep I agree. It's also crazy to me that in America golf courses take up the amount of space that Rhode Island and Delaware do when they are combined together
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Maybe the US should just make a state that is just one big golf course
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: That's not a bad idea! Also it's interesting that Babe Ruth was a famous golf player, we only hear about him as a famous baseball player
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Well I guess Babe's skill at swinging the bat transferred to swinging a golf club too
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Haha It would appear so! what do you think about Samuel L Jackson by the way? Apparently he has a built-in to his contract that he can play golf at least twice a week while he's making movies lol
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Samuel L Jackson must be a good golfer then with all the movies he stars in
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yes I would imagine so! Do you ever play tennis by the way? I played it a lot in high school
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I played once in high school, swinging a tennis racket and hitting the ball is a lot harder than it looks
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yes I agree you have to be very coordinated. It's also interesting that the polo shirt was invented by tennis player and not a polo player in my opinion
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yea, Rne Lacoste was the first person to wear polo shirts, crazy how popular they became
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yes and his nickname was the Crocodile and that is why polo Lacoste shirts have crocodiles on the logo LOL
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Pretty cool. Did you know Gael Monfils beat one of the best table tennis players a few days after he learned how to play?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Now that is cool. Apparently he beat the"Rodger Federer"of table tennis which is very impressive!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That is pretty cool, I wonder how long the match lasted, I know the longest tennis match was 11 hours long
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Guess you're right. And it lasted 3 days! Talk about exhausting, and that was back in 1969, it's interesting to that a black man won the US Open in 1968 but no black man has done the same since then
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Have any fitness goals for the year?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yes I do, I am thinking of followting the recomndations and start out with light cardio three to four times in a week, what about you? any goals
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Run a half-marathon soon. The trick to working up to it is consistency and increasing by at most 10 percent per week.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Thats great! how many km are in a half-marathon? I think its a good strategy to just add 10
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: 13.1. Doesn't hurt to vvary things up too. Throw some light cardio in there too, 20 minutes a pop three times a week.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yeah, because you can get injured if you start with more than three times as well, I think ill let soreness indicate when to increase weights or cardio.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah, it's not hard to get to 150 minutes a week if you take it easy and spread it out.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I know, and when you think about it it doesnt take much time just have to incorporate them as habits in your routine.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yep, the key is doing things that you can incorporate into a life long habit. DOn't compare your progress to others.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I agree, everyone has a different body so I am going to listen to mine and try to progress, getting faster, stronger.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Golf is a good low impact exercise. DO you golf?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: No, I have never known how to play, seems like a boring sport to me but I know many people are sick fans.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah not really my thing either. Although apparently Samuel Jackson loves it so much he has it in his contracts to let him play golf during productions.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Really? that must be really expensive, Babe Ruth was a golfer as well.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah apparently. I'm more of a guitar player myself. Brian May is not only a great guitarist but also an astrophysicist.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Brian May played/plays in Queen right? he must be one of the few that has a PHD.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah, he plays the guitar with a six pence coin. WHich is cool.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I see, one of my favorite guitarist is Eddie Van Halen.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: He's awesome. He apparently set his speakers on fire recording his solo for Michael Jackson's Beat It.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Thats crazy! so much power in those hands lol. Matthew Bellamy, the guitarist from muse hold the record for smashed guitar on a tour.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Seems like he needs some anger management. Maybe record an album in a nice calm place like the floating studio Astoria.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: He sure does, guitars will think about messing with him twice though lol and yeah I heard Pink Floyd recorded some themes there.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Hello, how are you? Do you like football?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I am well!  I watch a little here and there, you?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yes, I do too! Go to some games. That score in 1916 was crazy, 220-0! I am surprised they didn't stop it at some point!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I know, or at least run the clock, put that other team out of their misery.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: lol, I know. Are you interested in the planets?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I do find space fascinating.  There is so much to explore.  Even our near neighbor Mars.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Agree very fascinating!! Interesting they have a colonization project planned for 2023
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yeah, as a reality show.  I wonder how that is going to work out for them
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: funded by, I hope it works. lol I am not surprised to hear the sound on mars is at a higher pitch. Whenever I have heard the sounds of the planets it was always very high!!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I think it would be neat to travel there, it only takes 2 or so days under 1g acceleration.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: That's surprising, sounds easier when they put it that way
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Did you hear about the satellite they lost on mars?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I know, that imperial system doesn't work well.  Wonder where it went?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I know, did it fall off the edge?  Lol  I wonder if anyone ever thought mars was flat?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I don't know? They should have used Nasa's metric system. We have another rocket going up tomarrow here in the sunshine state!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Awesome.  Im a fellow Floridian!  Not near Kennedy though.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Oh really, great Enjoying our weather!!! They let one up already this week Did you see?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Nope.  I must have been inside.  The weather has been amazing this week, couldnt ask for better.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I know we are so lucky!! I would like to know how they use those flame throwers in cars to prevent carjacking? What they throw them at the people? Good chat!!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I dont know but it dosent sound like Ill be visiting soon.  LOL  You too!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Oh God no!! Something needs to be done there!! Have a good night!!
edus.speaker: agent_1
